narrative: OMX30 enters Friday in a technical consolidation phase: while the index maintains a structural bull market (7.6% above 200-MA), yesterday's MACD bearish crossover and neutral RSI suggest momentum is fading into overbought territory. Global correlation is weakly positive (+0.067%) but masks divergence—US equities rallied on Dow strength while Asia declined sharply (Nikkei -1.31%, Hang Seng -1.15%), signaling selective risk appetite rather than broad risk-on. The 10Y US yield fell 31bp, pressuring tech valuations and reducing refinancing relief for real estate. Copper weakness (-1.39%) and flat Brent (-0.68%) limit industrial and energy tailwinds. Key risk: Friday positioning ahead of weekend geopolitical headlines (see: Iran/Russia cyber intelligence story) could trigger late-session volatility.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens with modest positive bias on Dow momentum but faces technical headwinds. MACD bearish crossover is the primary red flag—watch for reversal below 3123 (yesterday close) which would crack the 50-MA support at 3180. ADX 22.1 confirms moderate trending environment, not explosive. Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo) lack tailwinds: USD/SEK down 0.41% removes export premium, copper down 1.39% erodes Boliden case, and oil flat doesn't help. Banks can offer slight refuge as 10Y volatility may eventually steepen on week-end risk, but don't chase here. Tech/Telecom should underperform (NASDAQ -0.09%, yields falling). Key watch: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for potential intraday volatility. Set stops tight at 3085 (lower Bollinger band). Bias is HOLD, not BUY.
drivers: Modest positive US equity signals (Dow +1.73%) offsetting Asian weakness
MACD bearish crossover and falling yields (-0.31%) pressuring growth narrative
OMX30 extended 7.6% above 200-MA with RSI 59.94 limiting upside momentum
Oil weakness and VIX uptick (15.7, +1.95%) suggesting risk-off sentiment building

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y fell 31bp to 4.477%, reducing near-term NIM expansion tailwind. Swedish rates likely follow but downside risk outweighs support today.
USD/SEK weakness (-0.41%), copper decline (-1.39%), and flat oil remove FX export boost and commodity input stability. Risk-off sentiment pressures capex spending expectations.
NASDAQ flat, yield decline compresses DCF valuations. Weak Asian markets (Nikkei -1.31%) signal demand softness in key customer regions.
Gold +0.31% provides minor tailwind but copper -1.39% is the dominant driver and it's negative. Mixed signals, lean HOLD.
Yields falling (-31bp) should be positive, but forward guidance uncertainty and VIX creep (15.7 +1.95%) signal refinancing risk and reduced leverage appetites.
EUR/SEK down (-0.17%) pressures sourcing costs for H&M. Risk-off sentiment and oil weakness point to consumer discretionary caution.

watchList.reason: Copper -1.39% outweighs gold +0.31%. Copper weakness typically signals demand destruction in growth-sensitive markets, conflicting with current risk-off setup.
Small positive bias from yield environment, but ADX 22.1 and lack of conviction in direction makes this a defensive hold, not a buy.
watchList.catalyst: ECB/Riksbank rate expectations, wider economic data

watchList.reason: USD/SEK weakness removes FX benefit. Industrial sentiment negative on copper/materials. Watch for break below 50-MA if broad OMX30 fails to hold.
NASDAQ flat, Asian weakness (Nikkei -1.31%) signals telecom/tech customer demand softness. Defensive positioning recommended.

keyLevels.resistance: 3145 (Bollinger upper half), 3165 (recent swing high before MACD crossover)
keyLevels.pivotWatch: 3123 (yesterday close) — break here on high volume = reversal signal

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims (Consensus: 237K) at 14:30 CET — main volatility catalyst today. Swedish calendar quiet. European PMI already priced in from earlier this week.
topRisk: Geopolitical escalation (Iran/Russia cyber intelligence story in press, SpaceX IPO uncertainty) could spike VIX past 18 and trigger cascade selling in OMX30. MACD already signaling momentum loss; any external shock finds thin buyers above 3145.
technicalSummary: BUT: MACD bearish crossover (hist=-0.832) is early-stage reversal warning
RSI 59.94 neutral, no overbought cushion for bad news
ADX 22.1 (moderate strength, still bullish bias but not conviction)
%B 0.59 = upper half of Bollinger bands, limited upside room
52W high -3.0%, suggests consolidation before next leg

newsHeadlines.title: Musk’s SpaceX lines up retail investors for record IPO allocation
newsHeadlines.description: Up to a quarter of the rocket builder’s $75bn float will be set aside for individual investors

newsHeadlines.title: The teenagers enlisted as agents of mayhem by Russia and Iran
newsHeadlines.description: Minors are being targeted to carry out hostile acts from Ukraine to western Europe and Israel

newsHeadlines.title: How much value is AI really creating?
newsHeadlines.description: Eye-opening changes to the speed and volume of work are not always translating into genuine productivity
